top_signals.title: U.S. Crude Inventories Post Another Major Draw
top_signals.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.1 million barrels during the week ending June 19, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The decrease brings commercial stockpiles to 412.1 million barrels, according to government data, which is now 7% below the five-year average for this time of year. The EIA’s data release follows API’s figures that were released a day earlier, which reported that crude oil inventories saw a draw of 765,000 barrels in the period.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Qualcomm stock pops 15% after chipmaker almost doubles projection for 2029 non-handset revenue
top_signals.summary: Qualcomm's primary business has been smartphones, which accounted for two-thirds of the company's product revenue in it's most recent quarter.

top_signals.title: Solar generation in CAISO surpassed natural gas in the first five months of 2026
top_signals.summary: In the first five months of 2026, utility-scale solar generation surpassed natural gas generation in CAISO. Solar electricity generation in the California Independent System Operator (CAISO) over the first five months of 2026 increased 21% compared with the same period in 2024, and natural gas generation decreased by 60%, data from our Hourly Electric Grid Monitor shows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
